varieties.name: Black Genoa
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red flesh, very sweet and juicy when well-ripened
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy cropper, 2 crops per year possible

varieties.name: Preston Prolific
varieties.traits: Yellow-brown skin turning purplish when ripe, large vigorous tree
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pale pink to amber flesh, sweet honey flavor with light lemon tang

varieties.name: Brown Turkey
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light pink flesh, mildly sweet with honey hints
varieties.growing_notes: Classic fig for subtropical regions. Thrives in Brisbane heat and humidity. Hardy and disease-resistant. Reliable producer. Used as rootstock in India for drought tolerance.
varieties.availability: Common in Australian nurseries, Daleys, Diggers Club AU

varieties.name: White Genoa
varieties.traits: Green to greenish-yellow skin, medium-large pyriform
varieties.flesh_fruit: Amber to reddish-pink flesh, tender and sweet with few seeds
varieties.growing_notes: Mild fig flavor suitable for those preferring less intense taste. Grows well in cooler Brisbane winters. Uniquely flavored with lemony notes. Two strains circulating in Australia.

varieties.name: Golden Genoa (Yellow Genoa)
varieties.traits: Yellow-green skin, similar to White Genoa
varieties.growing_notes: Australian subtropical adaptation. Grown in Brisbane region with success.

varieties.name: Excel
varieties.traits: Large yellow skin, blocky shape with no neck
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light amber flesh, very sweet, does not split
varieties.yield_notes: Early bearing, light breba crop, heavy main crop
varieties.growing_notes: Trialed at Narara NSW and recommended for subtropical Australia by Daleys. Well-adapted to Brisbane conditions. Excellent all-purpose fig. Split-resistant even in Brisbane humidity.
varieties.availability: Daleys Nursery Kyogle, Australian fig specialists

varieties.name: Blue Provence
varieties.traits: Bluish-purple skin with prominent ribs, medium fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Purple-tinged pulp with red seeds, very soft and sweet
varieties.growing_notes: Adapted to subtropical climates. Grown in Brisbane area. Note: Multiple strains exist; verification needed from Diggers Club source.
varieties.availability: Diggers Club AU (verify strain), specialty collections

varieties.name: Violette de Bordeaux
varieties.traits: Small jet-black fruit with frosty bloom, semi-dwarf naturally
varieties.flesh_fruit: Darkest strawberry-red flesh, rich complex berry flavors
varieties.yield_notes: Both breba and heavy main crop
varieties.growing_notes: One of finest tasting figs. Thrives in extreme heat AND humidity - perfect for Brisbane. Naturally small size ideal for small Brisbane gardens. Drought-tolerant once established.

varieties.name: Black Mission
varieties.origin: Spain (Balearic Islands) 1768, brought to California missions
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red flesh, rich sweet flavor
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for hot dry AND subtropical climates. Widely tested in Australia. Heat-tolerant and reliable. High sugar content for fresh eating or drying.

varieties.name: Celeste (Sugar Fig)
varieties.traits: Small-medium, bronzed to purple skin, dwarf tree (max 10ft)
varieties.yield_notes: Early ripening, large harvests, fruits first year

varieties.name: Kadota (Dottato)
varieties.origin: Italy (Roman times), commercial name from California
varieties.traits: Medium, thick light green-yellow skin, short neck
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light pink-amber flesh, nearly seedless, mild sweet honey flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable cropper, breba and main crop
varieties.growing_notes: Needs hot climate to ripen - Brisbane summers ideal. Good disease resistance including fig rust. Requires regular water during fruiting. Thrives in subtropical heat.

varieties.name: Panache (Tiger Fig)
varieties.traits: Striking green fruit with yellow stripes, medium size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Strawberry-red flesh, sweet with dry texture
varieties.growing_notes: Best for hot dry climates but adapts to Brisbane with proper care. Long warm Brisbane growing season suitable. Unique ornamental value. Requires hot summers.

varieties.name: Ronde de Bordeaux
varieties.traits: Small-medium dark purple fruit, exceptionally spherical
varieties.flesh_fruit: Reddish-pink flesh, sweet rich with grape-molasses hints
varieties.growing_notes: Similar to Violette de Bordeaux but smaller. Thrives in both dry heat and humidity. Good for Brisbane subtropical conditions. Semi-dwarf habit.

varieties.name: LSU Purple
varieties.traits: Medium, dark purple glossy skin, compact tree (8-10ft)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light strawberry-amber flesh, high sugar content
varieties.yield_notes: 3 crops: early spring, heavy summer, fall extending to December
varieties.growing_notes: SPECIFICALLY BRED FOR HOT HUMID CLIMATES - perfect Brisbane match. Superior disease resistance. Above average pest resistance. Excellent subtropical adaptation. Bred for Southern USA heat and humidity.
varieties.availability: Available from specialized importers to Australia

varieties.name: LSU Gold
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light red-amber interior, very sweet and juicy
varieties.growing_notes: Bred for hot humid Southern USA - excellent Brisbane adaptation. High disease resistance. Note: Sensitive to inconsistent moisture; needs regular watering in Brisbane rainy season.
varieties.availability: Available from specialized importers to Australia

varieties.name: Alma
varieties.growing_notes: Resistant to fig rust and anthracnose - important for Brisbane humidity. Heat-tolerant. Good for subtropical regions.

varieties.name: Peter's Honey
varieties.growing_notes: Performs well in hot dry AND humid climates. Heat-tolerant. Good Brisbane adaptation.

varieties.name: Conadria
varieties.origin: California (Ira Condit, 1950s) - White Adriatic × Capri fig
varieties.growing_notes: Performs well in hot climates. Good pest/disease resistance. Suitable for Brisbane heat. Introduced to India with good performance.

varieties.name: Chicago Hardy
varieties.origin: Mt. Etna type, Italian heritage via North America
varieties.traits: Medium, burgundy-purple skin, compact bushy growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light pink flesh, sweet strawberry-berry flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Early main crop, light breba (100+ figs possible)
varieties.growing_notes: While bred for cold climates, also heat-tolerant. Performs in Brisbane heat. Very productive and reliable. Container-friendly for Brisbane patios.

varieties.name: Deanna
varieties.growing_notes: Grown in Brisbane region successfully.

varieties.name: Doree
varieties.growing_notes: Adapted to Queensland subtropical conditions.

varieties.name: Dwarf Honey
varieties.traits: Compact slow-growing tree (1-1.5m max), brown skin

varieties.name: Pingo de Mel (Drop of Honey)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Fine-grained amber flesh, rich thick and sweet
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent for container growth in Brisbane. Mediterranean type that adapts to subtropical conditions.

varieties.name: Desert King
varieties.yield_notes: Mainly breba crop, light main crop
varieties.growing_notes: While named for desert, adapts to humid conditions. Large fruit size. Good for Brisbane with proper care.

varieties.name: Poona Fig 🏆
varieties.origin: Pune region, Maharashtra, India - indigenous Indian cultivar
varieties.traits: Medium size (20-30g), bell-shaped, thin skin, light purple
varieties.yield_notes: Most popular commercial variety in India, excellent productivity
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS INDIAN VARIETY. Most widely cultivated in Maharashtra and Karnataka. Adapted to Indian tropical/subtropical conditions. Suitable for fresh consumption and drying. Well-established in Indian monsoon climate.
varieties.availability: Widely available in Kerala nurseries, Alan Gardens Kottayam, throughout Maharashtra

varieties.name: Dinkar 🧬
varieties.origin: Marathwada Krishi Vidyapeeth, Parbhani, Maharashtra - Indian bred improvement
varieties.traits: Large fruit size, big sized fruit, reddish-pink pulp
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft reddish-pink pulp, sweet flavor, high TSS content
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding - 12 kg/plant, 200-300 fruits per tree
varieties.growing_notes: INDIAN BRED VARIETY - improvement over Daulatabad. Released by Indian agricultural university for commercial cultivation. Gaining popularity in Maharashtra. Excellent for fresh markets. Attractive appearance. Adapted to Indian conditions.
varieties.availability: Maharashtra nurseries, spreading to Kerala, Indian agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Purandar Fig (Local Poona type) 🏆
varieties.traits: Unique size, violet color from high calcium-potassium soil
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pulpy, special taste from sweet irrigation water
varieties.yield_notes: Fruits twice yearly (Do Bahar), 80% of Maharashtra production
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS HERITAGE VARIETY. Attributed to agro-climatic factors of Purandar region. Arid/semi-arid adaptation with moderate moisture. Legendary history dating to 1300s (Muhammad bin Tughlaq era). Threatened by imported varieties - conservation needed. Short shelf life vs imported types.
varieties.availability: Purandar region Maharashtra, limited commercial nurseries

varieties.name: Brown Turkey
varieties.growing_notes: Popular in India for tropical cultivation. Used as ROOTSTOCK in India for drought tolerance. Adaptable to Kerala monsoon conditions. Well-established in Indian horticulture.
varieties.availability: Common in Kerala nurseries, pan-India availability

varieties.name: Conadria
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light pink pulp, rich flavor, TSS 18° Brix
varieties.growing_notes: Performance found BETTER under Indian conditions. Introduced and tested in India. Heat and pest resistant. Good for tropical Kerala climate.

varieties.name: Excel
varieties.traits: Medium yellow fruit, oblate to spherical
varieties.flesh_fruit: Amber pulp, superb flavor, TSS 20° Brix
varieties.growing_notes: Introduced to India with success. Seems very hardy under Indian conditions. Drought-resistant. Excellent flavor profile for tropical cultivation.

varieties.name: Black Mission
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red flesh, rich sweet, high sugar
varieties.growing_notes: Popular for Indian cultivation. Heat-tolerant for Kerala temperatures. Excellent for drying - high sugar content. Adaptable to tropical conditions.
varieties.availability: Available in India through specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Calimyrna (Smyrna type)
varieties.growing_notes: Mentioned for Indian cultivation. Requires pollination via wasp. Excellent for drying. Adapted to hot climates.

varieties.name: Mission (Indian reference)
varieties.growing_notes: Listed in Indian agricultural literature. Common type fig classification. Suitable for tropical cultivation.

varieties.name: LSU Purple
varieties.growing_notes: SPECIFICALLY BRED FOR HOT HUMID CLIMATES - perfect Kerala monsoon match. Superior disease resistance. IDEAL for tropical monsoon Kerala. Above average pest resistance. Tested for Southern USA heat and humidity - directly applicable to Kerala.
varieties.availability: Can be sourced for India, specialty importers

varieties.name: LSU Gold
varieties.growing_notes: Bred for hot humid Southern USA - excellent Kerala adaptation potential. High disease resistance for monsoon conditions. Requires consistent moisture - suits Kerala monsoon. Heat-tolerant.
varieties.availability: Can be sourced for India, specialty importers

varieties.name: Israel Fig (Small Leaf)
varieties.traits: Average size, small leaves, slight red-black when ripe
varieties.yield_notes: Much better productivity, suitable for commercial plantation

varieties.name: Iranian/Persian Fig
varieties.traits: Various types adapted to tropical conditions
varieties.growing_notes: Mentioned as suitable for Kerala tropical forest situations. West Asian origin means heat-tolerant. Can adapt to Kerala monsoon with proper care.
varieties.availability: Alan Gardens Kottayam Kerala, specialty sources

varieties.name: Gular (Ficus racemosa)
varieties.traits: Small clustered fruits on trunk (cauliflorous), different species
varieties.flesh_fruit: Used for traditional medicine and culinary
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS INDIAN SPECIES (not Ficus carica). Also called Indian Fig or Cluster Fig. Native to subtropical, tropical and monsoonal rainforests. Grows wild in Kerala. Used in Ayurvedic medicine. Important biodiversity species. Found along watercourses.
varieties.availability: Wild in Kerala forests, some cultivation for medicinal use

varieties.name: Sandpaper Fig (Ficus coronata)
varieties.origin: Native to Australian subtropical/warm temperate (included as regional comparison)
varieties.traits: Small hairy purple-black fruit, sandpaper-like leaves
varieties.growing_notes: NATIVE SPECIES example (Australian). Subtropical to warm temperate adaptation. Mentioned for comparison of indigenous adaptation. Shows value of native fig species for local conditions.
varieties.availability: Australian native, not for Kerala cultivation

varieties.name: Alma
varieties.growing_notes: Resistant to fig rust and anthracnose - critical for Kerala monsoon humidity. Heat-tolerant. Disease resistance important for heavy monsoon.

varieties.name: Daulatabad 🌱
varieties.origin: Daulatabad, Maharashtra, India - historical Indian variety
varieties.growing_notes: HISTORICAL INDIAN VARIETY linked to 1300s Delhi Sultanate migration to Daulatabad. Heritage significance. Dinkar bred as improvement over this variety. Indigenous Indian cultivation history.

varieties.name: Timla Fig (YCD 1) 🌱
varieties.origin: Released 1993 from Horticultural Research Station Yercaud, Tamil Nadu
varieties.traits: Well adapted to rainfed situations, exceptionally hardy

varieties.name: Panachee (Tiger Stripe)
varieties.traits: Green with yellow stripes, pyriform with neck
varieties.flesh_fruit: Strawberry pulp, dry flesh but sweet
varieties.growing_notes: Unique appearance. Requires long warm season - Kerala provides year-round warmth. Heat-tolerant.

varieties.name: Violette de Bordeaux
varieties.yield_notes: Both breba and heavy main crop
varieties.growing_notes: Listed for humid climates. Thrives in extreme heat AND humidity - suitable for Kerala monsoon. Small size good for Kerala home gardens. CLOSED-EYE helps with humidity.

varieties.name: Peter's Honey (Kadota type)
varieties.growing_notes: Listed for humid climates. Performs well in both dry and humid. Heat-tolerant for Kerala temperatures.

varieties.name: Blanche (Blanch d'Argenteuil)
varieties.growing_notes: Heritage variety. Heat adaptation. Historical cultivation shows durability.
